original_abstract: The *ortho*-substituted phenyl ring is a fundamental structural motif in modern chemistry, featured in over three hundred commercial drugs and agrochemicals. Over the last decade, there has been a significant push to replace aromatic rings in bioactive molecules with saturated bioisosteres to discover novel, patentable chemical space. Despite this interest, efforts have largely focused on the replacement of *para*-substituted rings, leaving a gap in the design of *ortho* mimics. In this work, we present 2-oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes as saturated bioisosteres for the *ortho*-substituted phenyl ring. Crystallographic analysis confirms that these bicyclic scaffolds closely mirror the geometric properties of their aromatic counterparts. By replacing the phenyl rings in the commercial fungicides fluxapyroxad and boscalid with these bioisosteres, we demonstrate that the resulting analogues exhibit drastically improved water solubility and reduced lipophilicity while crucially maintaining their biological activity. These findings offer a robust strategy for medicinal and agrochemical researchers to optimize bioactive leads via the bioisosteric replacement of *ortho*-substituted phenyl motifs.
humanized_abstract: Ortho-Substituted Phenyl Rings Are Ubiquitous in Drugs and Agrochemicals and Have Attracted Increasing Attention in Recent Years as Targets for the Development of Saturated Bioisosteres. While there is great interest in replacing para-Substituted Aromatic Rings with Saturated Bioisosteres, far Less Attention Has Been Paid to Ortho-Substituted Phenyl Rings. In This Article, We Report the Development of 2-Oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes as Saturated Bioisosteres for Ortho-Substituted Phenyl Rings. X-Ray Crystal Structures of the Bicyclic Compounds Clearly Show That They Preserve the Stereochemistry of Their Aromatic Analogues. Metabolism Studies Revealed That the Known Fungicides Fluxapyroxad and Boscalid Can Be Modified by Replacing the Ortho-Substituted Phenyl Rings with the 2-Oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexane Bioisosteres. The Ortho-Substituted Phenyl Rings can be Replaced by 2-Oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes with a 17-Fold Increase in Water Solubility and a 2.3 Fold Decrease in Log D 7.4. At the Same Time, the Bioactivity of the Fungicides Is Retained. Thus, Ortho-Substituted Phenyl Rings in Bioactive Molecules Can be Replaced with Saturated Bioisosteres as a Means of Increasing the Diversity of Chemical Leads in Medicinal and Agrochemical Research.
